taxonomy_v3: Engineering and Development, Data Analysis, Data Scientist
core_skills: SQL, Statistics, Hypothesis testing, Strategic thinking
skill_match_scores: SQL:1.0, Statistics:1.0, Hypothesis testing:0.5, Strategic thinking:1.0
must_have_quals: Completed Bachelor's and/or Master's degree, ideally in a technical or quantitative field, 1.5+ years professional experience, Proficient with SQL, Basic understanding of statistics and hypothesis testing, Great communication skills, both written and verbal, Able to think strategically and tackle open-ended problems, Detail-oriented, organized, and a good team player
preferred_quals: Passion for problem-solving - If you've ever been so absorbed in a problem that your mind couldn't rest until you figured it out, you'll be in good company, Willing to help and be helped - Our impact comes only through helping others make better decisions using data. We recognize that we're stronger together - there's no shame in asking for help, we're not afraid to say 'I don't know', and we actively seek feedback, Desire to learn - You'll often be answering questions that have never been answered before, which requires a high level of intellectual curiosity and an eagerness to dive into new problems, domains, tools, and techniques
core_responsibilities: Work with senior leaders and other stakeholders within various departments at Lucid, Identify and understand problems and opportunities, Come up with data-driven recommendations, Implement new initiatives that help grow the business, Other duties as assigned
job_summary: Lucid Software is the leader in visual collaboration, helping teams see and build the future from idea to reality. As an Analyst on Lucid’s Strategy & Analytics team, you will engage in strategic problem solving, analyze data, present results, and drive change across various business functions.
